title: Secrets, GITHUB_TOKEN, Variables, Permissions, and Injection Defense · Release Evidence Brief: Ci Secret Variable Config Classification
summary: A workflow interpolates an issue title into shell, grants write-all globally, exposes a secret to an untrusted build, prints a derived credential, and mistakes masking for access control. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci secret variable config classification increment in workflow authority and data-flow policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify public configuration, non-secret variables, sensitive values, credentials, OIDC identity, and derived secrets by authority and lifetime.
Detect nondeterminism through repeated changed schedules, classify cause, quarantine with owner and expiry, and require repaired regression evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.

version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0

  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example job grants write-all, expands an issue title in a shell, prints a transformed secret, and sends a token to a mutable action. Reduce permissions, separate data from code, rotate exposure, and retain redacted evidence. Competency probe: Classify public configuration, non-secret variables, sensitive values, credentials, OIDC identity, and derived secrets by authority and lifetime. A defensible solution applies Ci Secret Variable Config Classification, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to workflow authority and data-flow policy.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-worked-gha-secrets-tokens-injection-4
  - competency: ci-worked-gha-secrets-tokens-injection-4
    artifact: authority-policy.yaml
    decision: worked gha secrets tokens injection 4
    environment: GitHub-hosted ubuntu-24.04 runner on a cold cache
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      default-permissions: none
      checkout-job: contents-read
      attest-job: contents-read-id-token-write-attestations-write
      public-config: repository-variable
      secret: environment-scoped
      untrusted-input: validated-env-boundary
      shell: no-generated-code
      derived-secret: explicitly-masked
      rotation: rehearsed
    verify: trace every value from event and secret store through expression environment shell process log artifact cache and API mutation
    failure: Reject write-all defaults, expression-to-shell interpolation, masking as access control, transformed-secret auto-masking, or log deletion as response.
    repair: Classify data, grant per-job authority, validate untrusted input, prevent sinks, mask derived values, revoke, rotate, and test absence.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
